stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/report_determinism_test adds the audit repro: two real runs of the same project whose killed-mutant stderr differs only in an ASLR pointer address (0x...) and an absolute source path now normalizeForComparison to identical bytes (before the fix this assertion failed because the raw addresses/paths survived into the report and normalizeForComparison ignores excerpts). A second unit test pins report.normalizeExcerpt: hex addresses become 0x<addr>, absolute path tokens become <path>, surrounding prose is kept, and two excerpts differing only in those survive normalization equal. Reverting boundedExcerpt to raw was confirmed to re-break the integration test.

stages.name: excerpt_determinism_all_modes
stages.evidence: Excerpt normalization is a pure text transform applied at capture, so it behaves identically in every optimize mode; the report_determinism_test cases pass in Debug, ReleaseSafe, and ReleaseFast. NOTE: ReleaseFast still surfaces the pre-existing, unrelated doctest_cli_command_test exit-code failure (recorded under task 107, flagged for a separate task); it is independent of this change.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: full self-mutation dogfood of zentinel's own runner/report is gated to the Phase 5 stages (task 085); the normalization fix is deterministic and proven by a behavioral repeated-run test that fails (reports not normalized-equal) without it.

residual_risk: Excerpt normalization redacts only hex pointer addresses (0x<hex>) and absolute path tokens (a /-rooted run at start-of-excerpt or after whitespace). Other potentially run-varying content in raw test output (e.g. decimal thread ids, PIDs, timestamps printed by a project's own tests) is NOT normalized; the audit finding and this task scope to addresses and absolute paths, and the report comparison still treats any remaining excerpt difference as a real difference. Projects whose test output prints such values can extend normalization in a follow-up.

An absolute path token is redacted whole (including any trailing :line:col), so the file/line inside a stack-trace path is not preserved in the excerpt; the diff and span fields still carry deterministic source location. This favors determinism over excerpt richness, per the task goal.

Normalization happens at capture (boundedExcerpt) rather than only in normalizeForComparison, so the stored report excerpt is already deterministic; normalizeForComparison and evidenceEqual consume the normalized bytes and need no excerpt-specific logic.
